title: Cleanup, Partial Initialization, and Error Paths · Failure Case: Failure Atomic Update
summary: A configuration loader acquires buffers, files, locks, and parsed objects before failures at each stage. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem failure atomic update increment in failure-atomic resource constructor. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Build replacement state separately and commit only after validation so callers retain a usable old value on failure.
Use structured goto cleanup when it centralizes unwinding without skipping initialization or obscuring ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Failure Atomic Update · Theory Failure Atomic Update
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 1739. Produce bounded C evidence to unwind partially acquired resources in reverse order; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
